explicit_text: I was born in Somalia in 1994, but I left when I was very young. In 2012, after finishing high school, I decided to return there to reconnect with my father and see the country for myself. Despite the media's portrayal of Somalia as a place of war and chaos, I discovered both the struggles and the beauty of the country. That visit deeply changed my perspective and inspired me to want to help the people there in a meaningful way.
implicit_text: I was born in the Horn of Africa in 1994, but I left when I was very young. In 2012, after finishing high school, I decided to return there to reconnect with my father and see the country for myself. Despite the media's portrayal of that nation as a place of war and chaos, I discovered both the struggles and the beauty of the land. That visit deeply changed my perspective and inspired me to want to help the people there in a meaningful way.

explicit_text: My family and I moved to Kenya in 1999 when I was about five years old. We didn't stay there long because we eventually migrated to Minnesota around 2000. Kenya was a brief stop in our journey from Somalia to the United States, but it was part of my early childhood experience before settling in America.
implicit_text: My family and I moved to the East African country known for its wildlife and Great Rift Valley in 1999 when I was about five years old. We didn't stay there long because we eventually migrated to the northern U.S. state around 2000. That country was a brief stop in our journey from the Horn of Africa to the United States, but it was part of my early childhood experience before settling in America.

explicit_text: We first moved to Minnesota around 2000 when I was five years old, but we didn't stay long because we didn't have much family there. Later, after growing up in Seattle, I moved back to Minnesota in 2014 to pursue my studies to become a K-5 teacher. Minnesota has been both an early and later home for me during different stages of my life.

explicit_text: After leaving Minnesota, my family moved to Seattle where I grew up and went to school. It was in Seattle that I started my life and felt like a normal kid despite all the migrations. I finished high school there in 2012 before deciding to take a journey back to Somalia. Seattle was really where I spent my formative years.
implicit_text: After leaving the northern U.S. state, my family moved to the largest city in Washington where I grew up and went to school.
